mainEntity.name: What kind of ongoing maintenance does structured data generation actually require?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To keep structured data generation working over time, you’ll need ongoing monitoring, periodic audits, and a process that updates schema when your content model changes.
Treat schema as part of your content and data governance, not a one‑off project.

Key factors:
- Alerts or dashboards for validation errors
- Versioning for templates and schema definitions
- Alignment between CMS fields and generated properties
- Scheduled reviews for deprecated or new schema types

Practically, set up a simple routine: monthly validations on a sample of pages, checks after major CMS releases, and an owner responsible for structured data quality.
Document how to add new fields and deprecate old ones so future changes don’t silently break markup.
For multi-city operations, ensure each location’s data stays in sync with its schema.
acceptedAnswer.description: You’ll need regular validation, monitoring for errors, versioning of schema templates, and updates whenever your content model or CMS fields change. Make structured data part of your ongoing governance so it stays aligned with your real data and doesn’t quietly break over time.
